title: Journal and atlas copy-budget sweep
summary: Keep the notebook rich but below the handheld text ceiling by pruning, not adding panels.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: Journal, atlas, comparison, and filed-note surfaces remain readable with compact relation-first summaries.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Add layout/budget tests or helpers for long journal/atlas strings without restructuring the notebook.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Run a copy-budget sweep over filed notes, ecosystem notes, atlas strips, and comparison summaries.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Provide screenshot proof for the most text-stressed notebook surfaces at 256x160.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Ensure route filing and notebook-ready displays inherit shortened text without losing route-state clarity.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

lane_1_scout_refinement.target: Add test-only copy-budget coverage for lane-1-owned station route, atlas, wrap, and active-outing resolver strings that can feed station, map, and quiet journal outing surfaces.
representative_states: coastal line logged / inland route opener with atlas present
inland line logged / edge-pattern opener with atlas present
edge line logged before Root Hollow
Root Hollow evidence counts 1, 2, 3, and notebook-ready
Root Hollow filed / Season Threads active
Season Threads filed / High Pass active
exclusions: Do not budget-lock routeBoard.notebookReady.previewText in lane 1; route filing and notebook-ready display inheritance belongs to packet 144 lane 4.

Do not edit src/content/**, ecosystem-note summaries, filed Route v2 note synthesis, comparison cards, close-look copy, science ledger entries, station layout, render coordinates, save schema, route-controller behavior, world-map behavior, corridor behavior, traversal behavior, or High Pass route-state behavior.

result: Added a small copy-budget helper and table-driven resolver coverage for lane-1 station route, atlas, wrap, and active-outing strings.

Covered representative saves from fresh beach through filed High Pass, including atlas-present route phases, Root Hollow evidence progress, Season Threads, High Pass active, High Pass ready-to-file, and High Pass filed.
Left routeBoard.notebookReady.previewText out of the budget guard for lane 4's route filing and notebook-ready display inheritance step.
preserved: No production runtime, station layout, render-coordinate, save-schema, route-controller, world-map, corridor, traversal, High Pass route-state, authored science content, ecosystem-note summary, filed Route v2 synthesis, comparison-card, close-look copy, or science-ledger changes.

confirmed: The implementation is test-only for runtime source and does not change station, atlas, journal, route, map, save, geometry, or content behavior.
The guard covers season-wrap, atlas note, route-board summary, launch-card copy, and active-outing summary/world-map/atlas strings across representative fresh-through-filed High Pass states.
routeBoard.notebookReady.previewText remains out of lane-1 scope for packet 144 lane 4.
lane_1_critic_result.scope_note: The shared worktree contains other pre-existing edits in src/test/field-season-board.test.ts; this review is scoped to the packet 144 lane-1 copy-budget helper and table-driven test.

lane_2_scout_refinement.target: Prune ecosystem-note summaries that can also surface as comparison-card summaries, while leaving atlas layout and route filed-note behavior untouched.

recommended_budget.reason: Comparison cards reuse ecosystem-note summaries inside a tighter handheld card than the normal journal detail surface.

recommended_copy.src/content/biomes/treeline.ts rime-foot: Rime favors low life on exposed High Pass ground, while tiny rock pockets give footholds.
recommended_copy.src/content/biomes/treeline.ts broken-ca: Under the last mountain trees, low floor plants mix with shrubs and brighter wind.
recommended_copy.src/content/biomes/forest.ts forest-floo: Near the last big trees, cool shade still stacks low leaves and tiny tree starts.
recommended_copy.src/content/biomes/forest.ts edge-berry-: The first forest edge stacks thorny stems and berries into a shadier thicket.
recommended_copy.src/content/biomes/coastal-scrub.ts pine: Under shore pines, low mats and quiet cover show steadier sand than open scrub.

atlas_audit.decision: Do not edit field-season-board or atlas rendering in lane 2; lane 1 owns broader atlas layout or helper work.

preserve: No edits to game, overlay-render, field-season-board, field-season-wrap, station state, route board layout, save, route controller, world map, corridor, or traversal behavior.
No route filed-note rewrites in this slice; packet 133 already added filed-note compactness and relationship-anchor guards.
No new notebook, atlas, comparison, HUD, or station panel.
No science-source-ledger churn unless a touched claim changes meaning.

result: Lowered NOTE_SUMMARY_MAX from 110 to 96 and renamed the test to protect both journal notes and comparison-card copy.
Trimmed the five named ecosystem-note summaries while preserving note ids, entry ids, unlock rules, zones, observation prompts, and science meaning.

Left atlas rendering/layout, station state, route-board layout, route-controller behavior, save behavior, world-map behavior, corridor behavior, traversal behavior, and route filed-note copy unchanged.

confirmed: NOTE_SUMMARY_MAX is 96 and the content-quality test names the journal plus comparison-card budget.
The five packet-scoped ecosystem-note summaries are all under the new cap: rime-footholds 89, broken-canopy-floor 82, forest-floor-carpet 81, edge-berry-thicket 77, and pine-underlayer 79.
Touched note ids, entry ids, unlock rules, zones, observation prompts, and science meaning stayed intact.

Atlas rendering/layout, station state, route-board layout, route-controller behavior, save behavior, world-map behavior, corridor behavior, traversal behavior, route filed-note logic, and new UI scope stayed untouched for this packet.
lane_2_critic_result.scope_note: The shared worktree still contains unrelated earlier lane edits in some of the same files; this review is scoped to packet 144's five named summary trims plus the budget/test-name update.

lane_3_scout_refinement.target: Provide visual proof, not new notebook code: capture the most text-stressed journal comparison/detail and field-station atlas states at the live 256x160 viewport.

recommended_surfaces.setup: Custom Treeline journal save with mountain-avens sighted in treeline+tundra, moss-campion in treeline, and woolly-lousewort in tundra.
recommended_surfaces.expected_state: journal.selectedBiomeId treeline; journal.selectedEntryId mountain-avens; ecosystem note Fell Bloom Window; comparison available but closed

recommended_surfaces.setup: Same save, then open comparison with C/Enter.
recommended_surfaces.expected_state: comparison open with Fell Bloom Window and Brief Thaw Bloom cards

recommended_surfaces.setup: Existing high-pass-ready-to-file debug snapshot opened to field station SEASON routes page.
recommended_surfaces.expected_state: fieldStation.view season; atlas non-null; route board and atlas strip readable together

preserve: No authored science copy, route filed-note synthesis, station state, save schema, world-map behavior, corridor behavior, traversal geometry, High Pass route logic, or new UI panels.
If screenshots reveal copy overflow, hand off to lane 2; if station layout or route-preview behavior is at fault, hand off to lane 1 or lane 4.
verification: Run the required web-game client at least once.
Capture screenshots plus render_game_to_text JSON states under output/lane-3-main-384-browser/.

result: Completed the lane-3 visual proof with no production source changes.
Confirmed the alpine Mountain Avens journal detail state remains contained, while the comparison-open state presents Fell Bloom Window and Brief Thaw Bloom cards without overlap.
Confirmed both High Pass ready-to-file and filed station atlas states keep the route board plus FIELD ATLAS strip readable at 256x160.
preserved: No authored science copy, route filed-note synthesis, station state, save schema, world-map behavior, corridor behavior, traversal geometry, High Pass route logic, or new UI panel changed.
verification: Required web-game client run against http://127.0.0.1:4174
Focused Playwright screenshots plus render_game_to_text states under output/lane-3-main-384-browser/

confirmed: The lane-3 pass is proof-only and did not change production runtime, content, station, route, save, corridor, traversal, High Pass, or UI-panel behavior.
The alpine journal detail state is tight but contained, and the comparison-open state presents Fell Bloom Window and Brief Thaw Bloom cards without visible overlap.
High Pass ready-to-file and filed station atlas states keep the route board plus FIELD ATLAS strip readable at 256x160.
Companion render_game_to_text states match the expected journal selection, comparison cards, field-station route page, and atlas notes.
verification: jq state checks for journal detail, journal comparison, station ready atlas, station filed atlas, and empty browser errors
Prior implementation proof includes required web-game client and focused Playwright captures

lane_4_scout_refinement.target: Add test-only station notebook-ready preview budget coverage so route filing displays inherit shortened filed-display text without losing canonical route labels.
exclusions: Keep forest-expedition-upper-run and treeline-high-pass out of routeBoard.notebookReady budget coverage because they use expedition and High Pass chapter seams instead of the season-route notebookReady bridge.

Do not edit production runtime, route filed-note copy, broad content, station layout, save schema, world-map behavior, corridor or traversal behavior, science ledger entries, support behavior, or High Pass chapter-state wiring unless a focused test exposes a real bug.
baseline_verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "filed-note synthesis matrix|High Pass|Thaw Window"

result: Added a behavior-neutral table-driven station notebook-ready preview guard for the nine board-mapped ready Route v2 notes.

Asserted preview labels remain canonical and <= 24 characters, preview text stays <= 144 characters, note-tabs wrap inherits the same preview text, and default wraps keep NOTEBOOK READY plus route ready text.
Kept Short Season as the filing label while allowing the Thaw Window display prefix in the preview text.
preserved: No production runtime, route filed-note synthesis, route definition, station layout, save schema, world-map, corridor, traversal, support, content, science-ledger, Root Hollow expedition, or High Pass chapter-state changes.
forest-expedition-upper-run and treeline-high-pass remain on their existing expedition and High Pass chapter seams instead of routeBoard.notebookReady.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "filed-note synthesis matrix|High Pass|Thaw Window"

confirmed: The implementation is behavior-neutral and adds only station notebook-ready preview budget coverage.
The table covers the nine board-mapped ready Route v2 notes from beach-shore-shelter through treeline-low-fell.
Preview labels remain canonical and <= 24 characters, preview text remains <= 144 characters, and note-tabs wrap text inherits the same preview text.
Default non-note-tabs wraps keep NOTEBOOK READY plus route ready text.
SHORT SEASON remains the filing label while Thaw Window. stays display-prefix preview context.
forest-expedition-upper-run and treeline-high-pass remain on the existing expedition and High Pass chapter seams.
lane_4_critic_result.scope_note: The shared worktree contains unrelated dirty files from other lane work; this review is scoped to packet 144 lane 4's route preview guard and handoff surfaces.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "filed-note synthesis matrix|High Pass|Thaw Window"
